title: wedelia rugosa
intro: Wedelia rugosa, also known as flowering spurge or Mexican daisy, is a flowering plant native to Mexico and Central America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Wedelia rugosa include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Wedelia rugosa can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, and is often used in Ayurvedic and traditional Mexican medicine.
